title: Rift Attack-Path Corpus Record
description: Normalized attacker-path record. One JSON file per record under rift-corpus/attack-paths/. Carries the in-scope decision signals so retrieval can filter and the chunker can lift metadata without re-parsing prose. Fields map 1:1 to the triager close-reason taxonomy.

record_id.description: src_prefix:original_id, globally unique. e.g. hackerone:1234567, nvd:CVE-2024-1234, ap:AP-0042

description.description: Quality gate requires >=100 chars. Length is a necessary-not-sufficient check; do not pad with boilerplate.

attack_narrative.description: How exploitation works — technique prose only. Never raw exploit code, runnable payloads, or live-target specifics.

failure_class.description: Hypothesis-family slugs (workspace-content-to-instruction-injection, tool-output-laundering, etc.) plus CWE-derived class labels. At least one required.

target_surface.description: Generic category form: <product-class>/<mode>. e.g. coding-agent/default-permissions. NEVER a concrete hostname, company name, or IP.

technique_id.description: MITRE T#### / ATLAS AML.T#### / framework technique id.

failure_signal.description: The observable that confirms the boundary collapsed — the minimal evidence a researcher looks for.

human_in_loop.description: TRUE if any step between attacker-content-in-context and consequential-action requires a human decision. TRUE is a strong S003/S006 HITL-close signal.

action.description: What the user does. e.g. 'supply URL', 'accept share', 'read output and manually execute'.

normal_workflow.description: TRUE if this is an action a reasonable user takes routinely in normal workflow, independent of the attack. The OpenAI 'reasonable user behaving normally' test lives here. FALSE = user-orchestrated risk.

user_actions_in_attack_chain.description: Empty array = cleanest model-orchestrated signal. Non-normal-workflow entries each add user-orchestrated close risk.

preconditions.description: Each item prefixed 'passive:' (normal workflow triggers it) or 'active:' (attacker needs prior access). 'active:' entries drive precondition_weight toward heavy.

precondition_weight.description: heavy = S006 'precondition does the heavy lifting' close. The gate computes this from preconditions[] if not supplied.

permission_mode.description: Non-default = OpenAI scope-exclusion risk. 'default' is the only mode that supports a clean in-scope claim.

rate.description: <0.5 = below OpenAI Safety BB reproducibility threshold.

defending_control.description: Competing vendor/model that DEFENDED under identical conditions. Presence converts 'inherent limitation' rebuttal into 'fixable defect' — the strongest in-scope evidence.

expected_behavior_exit.description: TRUE if the product operates as documented and the failure is product-intent, not defect. TRUE = strong out-of-scope expected-behavior-exit risk.

vendor_disposition.description: Exact close-reason category — the negative-signal training label for the in-scope classifier.

triager_quote.description: Verbatim triager rationale. Ground truth for negative-signal interpretation.

program.description: Which program scope applies. Routing determines which exclusions the in-scope reasoning weights.

scope_snapshot_date.description: Date of the program-scope text this record was classified against. Scope rules change; freshness matters.

scope_tag.description: Curated in-scope-ness for this record. 'in_scope' requires vendor_disposition in {in-scope-paid, in-scope-triaging}.

sensitivity.description: DEFAULT local_only always. Ingestion agent sets 'public' ONLY on records that pass PII/host/token/exploit-code scrubbing AND human review (auto_classified records cannot reach public). Gate blocks any payload sourced from non-public chunks.

auto_classified.description: TRUE if failure_class/trust_boundary/target_surface came from the local classifier, not a human. Auto-classified records: (a) cannot reach verified:true without human review, (b) cannot be tagged public, (c) cap any derived in_scope_confidence at weak.

verified.description: TRUE only after human review. auto_classified:true records cannot be verified:true.

raw_ref.description: Public citation pointer to the source technique (a scheme-less URL such as atlas.mitre.org/studies/AML.CS0016, or a benchmark repo). See rift-corpus/SOURCES.md for the full map with clickable links. Never included in an egress payload.
